data.epi_content:
The ceiling.

On the pristine white ceiling hangs a chandelier—ornate yet relatively small.

Around the chandelier, wave-like patterns are drawn, resembling clouds.

When looking up at the sky, if something sky-like blocks your view, it feels somewhat suffocating.

If it were just painted pure white, that would be one thing, but this mediocre painting feels deceptive.

I don't like it.

Come to think of it, the burning sensation I felt when breathing in and out is gone.

Curiously, I touch my neck and feel a slight surprise at how completely healed it seems.

And as the surprise fades, what follows is suspicion.

Who brought me here? Where exactly is this place?

Why am I even here to begin with?

After enduring Lydia's cruel bullying, I tried to make my way back to my room despite my exhaustion.

"Cruel" might be putting it too mildly.

But at least I remember that much clearly.

Then someone blocked my path, and I collapsed from weakness.

And when I opened my eyes, I was here.

I gather the comfortably draped blanket, set it aside, and get up.

My body feels fresh and clean.

There's no trace of body odor, and my clothes are perfectly fine despite the cold sweat I must have broken into.

Perhaps a maid who was cleaning the street picked me up?

Could a maid use healing magic...? Maybe.

I'm not sure. I doubt the setting was developed in such detail.

Perhaps there are ordinary people who learn it too.

It's not particularly important.

What I notice next is the rather fragrant coffee aroma.

The distinctive scent of freshly roasted and ground beans fills the room.

When I turn my head, there's a cup of rich coffee on the nearby table that makes my mouth water just from the smell.

Due to my financial situation, I haven't been able to properly buy beans or drink coffee.

But I decide to pass on it, not knowing what might be in it.

"What's going on?"

Since I've never experienced anything like this before, if I think about it rationally, was I kidnapped?

If so, I'm being treated quite well.

There are no silver bracelets restricting my movements on my wrists or ankles, nor am I tied up with a leash somewhere.

The bookshelf is filled with books, and the room looks like any ordinary dormitory room, so leaving shouldn't be difficult.

So, someone must have brought me here.

I must have looked quite ill.

Well, I was drenched in cold sweat and so exhausted that even breathing was difficult.

When will this frail body ever get better?

I could sit on the bed and ramble on with metaphysical nonsense like "who am I?" or "is what I'm seeing real?" but wanting to leave this place, I put on the slippers neatly placed on the floor and walk toward the entrance.

Come to think of it, the pain from the wounds on my feet is gone too.

The maid, or whoever it was, must have used healing magic on my feet as well.

How embarrassing—bare feet give off a somewhat indecent and unpleasant feeling.

I examine the books on the bookshelf in the room.

They're all about magic.

Books filled with what looks like alien language that I wouldn't understand even if someone opened them for me.

"You're awake? I was really shocked when you suddenly collapsed."

And with that voice, all my questions are answered as I look in its direction.

A man taller than me by a head, with a pale face that suggests he's spent all his time studying indoors rather than getting any sunlight, walks out from an inner room—a scholarly-looking fellow.

It's tedious to describe his face in detail, but to put it simply, he's quite handsome.

Eyes where eyes should be, nose where a nose should be—that kind of thing.

By the way, this means Vivian might not end up with him in the end.

And apparently, there are at least three more guys like him hanging around Vivian.

I should have read all the way to the end.

The story was so childish and lacking in plausibility that a perfectly respectable noble family could collapse overnight.

At some point, it became so boring that I couldn't even finish it. Haha.

And here I am, living in that world.
data.epi_content2:
Evan, seemingly confused by my lack of response, scratches the back of his head slightly before putting his hands in his pockets.

"...Ah. Evan."

"The coffee on the table is the kind you like.

I just brewed it, so it should still be warm. If you want it cooler, I can add some ice."

His awkward smile as he tries to continue the conversation feels like a jerky puppet.

I'm not sure what he's trying to say, but he keeps opening his mouth slightly and then closing it again.

So after I slapped Vivian's cheek, comforted her when she was miserable, then the next day she came to me with harsh words, and the day after that my family collapsed without any plausibility, and then this young lady went somewhere to the other world, and now I'm here—is that how it feels?

No wonder it's awkward.

Anyway, I sit back on the bed, pick up the coffee cup, and first smell it.

I touch the cup to my lips to gauge the temperature, then take a sip of the black liquid.

Enjoying the slightly bitter taste and aroma in my mouth, I swallow it lightly.

"It's delicious."

"Really? It's what you recommended to me before, saying it was delicious."

"I haven't had anything like this for a while."

After that, only the sound of me sipping coffee could be heard in the silence.

An uncomfortable and awkward relationship.

"I heard about your father... yesterday.

And I read about what happened to your family in the newspaper."

Evan has always been like this.

No tact, just bringing up whatever topic comes to mind.

I don't know how to respond.

So I just nod once in affirmation.

As if to say, please don't bring up this topic and just move on.

Whatever happened to my father, or when he heard about it, what does it matter?

It would be nice if he offered some comfort. But he probably won't.

"Yeah."

Before meeting Vivian, and even after meeting her until this young lady showed jealousy when she noticed Evan's feelings for Vivian, we were very close.

We were childhood friends who visited each other often.

Sometimes I even dream of scenes of playing with Evan, or this young lady falling for him.

I myself have no interest or curiosity about Evan, but this young lady seems to still be in love with the pale scholarly man in front of me.

There's still a slightly painful feeling in every word.

Because of this, I think I should stop distinguishing between this young lady and myself, but how easy could that be?

Time will solve everything. Though I'm not sure if I have the mental strength to endure until then.

"Thank you for helping me."

Evan responds by shrugging his shoulders.

Come to think of it, the reason I collapsed was because Evan blocked my path and wouldn't move.

I heard him mumbling something, but I was too exhausted to hear properly.

"Were you looking for me?"

"Because of what happened with Vivian last time, I felt like I said some harsh things in anger, so I was looking for you to apologize.

Vivian also said she was worried about you."

"Why, did you think I was holed up for days because of you?"

My tone becomes sharp without me realizing it.

I didn't think I was an emotional person.

This young lady must be quite sensitive.

What can I do if I just become like this whenever I'm in front of Evan?

Anyway, despite my frosty words, the man answers calmly without blinking an eye.

"Yeah."

"That's part of it. But you said you read the newspaper.

I was just a little sad. Because everything I had is gone. Including you.

You know why I dislike Vivian."
data.epi_content3:
"..."

"I'm joking."

Even though I said it's as a joke, the atmosphere doesn't feel like a joke at all.

That's paradoxically what makes it... well, I shouldn't have brought it up.

When I finish my coffee and put down the cup, Evan brings a transparent glass pot and pours me more.

While I stare at the rising steam for a while, he casually throws out a question.

"But if you're hurt... your mouth and throat were all burned earlier. Why is that?"

What excuse could I give?

Since it's a burn injury, there's probably no scar, but I indirectly convey that I don't want to answer, even with a lie.

"Well, maybe I burned my palate and esophagus drinking hot coffee."

"There were scabs on the wounds on your feet too."

"I must have stepped on thorns by mistake."

He frowns at that.

"If you're hurt, at least get treatment."

"From whom?"

"You could ask those friends you hang out with. Lydia, was it?

That girl who was always clinging to you so familiarly. Why did you go to Vivian..."

He cuts off his words.

Anyway, is there no problem?

I wasn't dragged here by a stranger, I don't seem likely to be harmed, and we just don't communicate well.

And the solution to the communication problem is to avoid conversation and escape to the cozy room.

"Right, why didn't I do that?

We were laughing and playing together just at lunch.

Oh, look at me, haha."

They say there are many motivations for extreme choices.

Heartbreak or incurable diseases—these things seem plausible when reading about them in newspapers, but has anyone ever investigated whether a friend of a desperate person responded with an indifferent tone? That person would be the culprit, they say.

That's quite a resonant statement.

The dam that held back my emotions cracked with Lydia's petty bullying and then collapsed.

And thanks to my conversation with Evan just now, all the resentment and boredom that had been in a state of suspension began to rush in at once.

He glanced at me and said in a small voice.

"If you really have no one to ask, tell me."

"You?"

"Because I'm worried."

Ah, that's kind of sweet.

I must look a bit unstable.

"Hehe."

Hearing that he's worried, I smiled without realizing it.

Though Evan looks rather frightened.

It's the first time this young lady has heard such words.

It's the first time Evan has said such things to me.

I'm not in a position to be worried about in the first place. Ah, no, I wasn't.

"I'm going to leave. Thanks for the coffee."

As I get up, feeling a bit dizzy, I walk toward the entrance.

And while I'm putting on my shoes, I hear Evan's voice.

"I'll make you coffee anytime, so come by if you want some."

Without answering, I left the room.

With his intense gaze on the back of my head.

Remembering the superficial exchanges we had, I recall what Vivian said.

This is what it means to miss each other.

Because we know but do nothing about it.
